# PyData Johannesburg Operating Model

## 2.1 Core Principles

PyData Johannesburg operates on four foundational principles that guide all decisions and activities:

### Community First
- **Member needs drive decisions**: Event topics, timing, and format respond to community feedback
- **Inclusive by design**: Actively work to reduce barriers to participation  
- **Value contribution over consumption**: Encourage active participation from attendees
- **Shared ownership**: Community members have agency in shaping direction

### Lightweight Governance
- **Minimal bureaucracy**: Keep processes simple and avoid unnecessary complexity
- **Distributed decision-making**: Empower organizers to act within agreed frameworks
- **Transparent operations**: Share decision-making rationale with the community
- **Iterative improvement**: Regularly review and adjust processes based on outcomes

### Professional Standards
- **Quality content**: Maintain high standards for talks and workshops
- **Respectful environment**: Enforce code of conduct consistently and fairly
- **Industry relevance**: Balance learning opportunities with practical applications
- **Reliable operations**: Deliver consistent, well-organized events

### Industry + Open Source Collaboration
- **Bridge building**: Connect corporate users with open source contributors
- **Knowledge flow**: Facilitate learning in both directions between industry and projects
- **Sustainable partnerships**: Build mutually beneficial relationships with sponsors
- **Global connection**: Link local community to international developments

## 2.2 Organisational Structure

PyData Johannesburg follows a **guild model** with distributed leadership rather than a single organizer handling all responsibilities. This structure ensures sustainability, prevents burnout, and enables specialization.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    Lead[Community Lead]
    
    Lead --> Events[Events Team]
    Lead --> Speakers[Speaker Pipeline Team]
    Lead --> Partnerships[Industry Partnerships]
    Lead --> Universities[University Outreach]
    Lead --> Marketing[Marketing & Community]
    Lead --> Infrastructure[Infrastructure Team]
    
    Events --> EventCoord[Event Coordinator]
    Events --> Volunteers[Event Volunteers]
    
    Speakers --> SpeakerCoord[Speaker Coordinator]  
    Speakers --> Mentors[Speaker Mentors]
    
    Partnerships --> CorpRel[Corporate Relations]
    Partnerships --> Venues[Venue Partners]
    
    Universities --> AcademicOut[Academic Outreach]
    Universities --> StudentEng[Student Engagement]
```

### 2.2.1 Lead Organiser
**Primary responsibility**: Overall community strategy and coordination

**Key activities**:
- Set strategic direction for community growth
- Coordinate between different organizer teams
- Manage relationships with NumFOCUS and global PyData network
- Handle escalated issues and conflicts
- Represent community externally

**Time commitment**: 3-5 hours per week
**Term**: 12-18 months with succession planning

### 2.2.2 Community Organisers (2-3 people)
**Primary responsibility**: Day-to-day community engagement and member experience

**Key activities**:
- Respond to community questions and feedback
- Facilitate discussions in community channels
- Identify emerging topics and speakers
- Support new member onboarding
- Monitor community health metrics

**Time commitment**: 2-3 hours per week
**Term**: 6-12 months with flexible rotation

### 2.2.3 Event Coordinator
**Primary responsibility**: End-to-end event execution

**Key activities**:
- Schedule and coordinate with venues
- Manage event logistics (catering, AV, setup)
- Handle registrations and attendee communications
- Coordinate day-of-event operations
- Collect and analyze event feedback

**Time commitment**: 4-6 hours per month, 8+ hours during event weeks
**Term**: 6-12 months

### 2.2.4 Speaker Coordinator
**Primary responsibility**: Speaker pipeline and content quality

**Key activities**:
- Source and evaluate potential speakers
- Support speaker preparation and content development
- Manage speaker communications and scheduling
- Maintain speaker database and feedback systems
- Develop relationships with companies for employee speakers

**Time commitment**: 3-4 hours per week  
**Term**: 12-18 months (relationship building benefits from continuity)

### 2.2.5 Marketing & Community Manager
**Primary responsibility**: Community growth and external communications

**Key activities**:
- Manage social media presence and event promotion
- Create and distribute marketing materials
- Build relationships with media and other communities
- Track and analyze community growth metrics
- Coordinate with sponsors on promotional activities

**Time commitment**: 2-3 hours per week
**Term**: 6-12 months

### 2.2.6 Infrastructure Maintainer
**Primary responsibility**: Technical systems and digital assets

**Key activities**:
- Maintain community websites and online presence
- Manage community communication platforms
- Handle video recording and publishing workflows
- Maintain speaker and event databases
- Ensure data privacy and security compliance

**Time commitment**: 2-4 hours per month, more during system updates
**Term**: 12+ months (technical knowledge accumulates over time)

## 2.3 Organiser Expectations

### Time Commitment
Organizing requires consistent, sustainable effort rather than intense bursts:
- **Minimum viable**: 2 hours per week for most roles
- **Optimal performance**: 3-5 hours per week for lead roles
- **Event weeks**: Expect 50-100% increase in time requirements
- **Flexibility**: Workload varies by season and community growth phase

### Responsibilities
All organizers are expected to:
- **Attend monthly organizer meetings** (1 hour, virtual)
- **Participate in 80% of community events** (either in person or virtual)
- **Respond to community communications** within 48 hours during business days
- **Complete assigned pre-event tasks** at least 1 week before events
- **Provide feedback and improvement suggestions** after each event

### Professional Conduct
- **Represent PyData values** in all community interactions
- **Maintain confidentiality** around sensitive community or sponsor information
- **Declare conflicts of interest** when they arise
- **Support fellow organizers** and share workload during busy periods
- **Follow escalation procedures** for difficult situations

### Promotion and Outreach
- **Personal networks**: Leverage professional connections to identify speakers and attendees
- **Industry presence**: Attend other data events to build relationships and represent PyData
- **Content creation**: Contribute blogs, social posts, or talks about community activities
- **Mentorship**: Support new organizers and community members
